title: Dwarf Scenario v1
description: Forensically-sound test, security-test, and fuzzing scenario for Cardano implementations. A scenario is data; primitives are code. The runner instantiates primitives by name from primitives/registry.json and refuses references to unknown primitives or to primitives whose declared runtimes do not include the scenario's runtime.

spec_version.description: Spec major version. v1 scenarios validate against this schema. Breaking changes go to v2 in a new directory.

id.description: Globally unique scenario identifier. Lowercase kebab-case. Stable across renames; treat as a primary key.

title.description: Human-readable one-line title shown in dashboards and reports.

authors.description: Optional list of author handles or names.

tags.description: Optional free-form tags for indexing and filtering (e.g. networking, peer-selection, abuse, ser-deser).

related_milestones.description: Optional milestone labels this scenario supports, such as M2.

m1_trace.description: Optional traceability back to Milestone 1 artifacts. Keys are trace groups such as threat_ids, gap_ids, architecture_ids, and risk_candidate_ids; values are ID lists.

evidence_intent.description: How this scenario's evidence is intended to be used. This does not promote or score a finding by itself.

promotion_blockers.description: Human/code/test gates that must be closed before this scenario supports a promoted finding or risk decision.

testcase_candidate.description: Optional instruction to ingest the completed run as a testcase lifecycle record automatically. Useful for runtime/devnet anomaly families that do not start from a byte-level corpus input.

target.description: Which Cardano implementation this scenario targets. The runner refuses if any referenced primitive does not declare support for this implementation.

version.description: Version constraint. 'any' accepts whatever is deployed; otherwise a semver range, branch, or commit hash. Resolved value is recorded in the run manifest.

runtime.description: Execution tier. 'library' drives a library or binary harness directly (no node, no devnet). 'single-node' spins up one node process. 'devnet' deploys a multi-node devnet via the profile-manager. The runner refuses if any referenced primitive's declared runtimes do not include this value.

profile.description: Devnet profile id, required when runtime is 'devnet', forbidden otherwise. Resolved profile content is captured in the run bundle.

duration.description: Optional wall-clock cap on the load phase. Accepted units: s, m, h. Examples: '30s', '10m', '2h'.

seed.description: Optional integer or hex string used to seed all primitives' RNGs. If omitted, the runner generates one and records it in the run manifest. Required for deterministic replay.

setup.description: Ordered list of setup primitive references. Failures abort the run before load.

load.description: Ordered list of load primitive references. The first load primitive's duration (or the scenario's duration field) bounds the run.

faults.description: Optional fault-injection primitives executed concurrently with load.

probes.description: Probe primitives sampled concurrently with load. Each probe writes its time series to probes/<primitive>.ndjson in the run bundle.

assertions.description: Assertion primitives evaluated after load completes. Each assertion records its evaluated value, the data points used, and a pass/fail result in the run bundle.

teardown.description: Ordered list of teardown primitive references. Run regardless of pass/fail. Failures are recorded but do not change the scenario's overall pass/fail.

iterations.description: Number of trial iterations to run when this scenario contains FUZZ slots. Required at runtime when any primitive parameter is the bare keyword "FUZZ" or the long-form object {"fuzz": {...}}. Defaults to 100 when omitted. Each iteration becomes its own child bundle under a parent run id; all iterations share the parent's chain entry plus a chain entry of their own.

shrink.description: When true (default), failing iterations trigger shrinking: the runner attempts to find a smaller failing input by reducing FUZZ-generated values toward minimum bounds (smaller integers, shorter strings/bytes). The shrunk failing input is recorded alongside the original. Shrinking only runs when an iteration fails, so it is free in the happy path.

allOf.description: profile is required when runtime is devnet, forbidden otherwise.

primitive_ref.description: Reference to a primitive registered in primitives/registry.json. The 'primitive' key names the primitive; remaining keys are parameters validated against that primitive's params_schema. Any parameter value may be a literal matching the primitive's params_schema, OR the bare keyword string "FUZZ" (the runner generates values from the primitive's declared parameter type), OR a long-form fuzz spec object {"fuzz": {...}} (see fuzz_slot below). The runner enforces FUZZ semantics; this scenario schema accepts any value here so primitives can declare arbitrary parameter shapes.

primitive.description: Primitive name as registered in primitives/registry.json. Must exist; the runner refuses unknown names.

fuzz_slot.$comment: Documentary schema for the long-form FUZZ slot object. Not directly referenced by primitive_ref (parameter values are free-form) but defines the contract the FUZZ engine uses when it sees {fuzz: {...}} as a parameter value. The bare string "FUZZ" is shorthand for {fuzz: {type: <inferred from primitive params_schema>}}.

type.description: Generator strategy. Supported in Slice 8.5: int | bytes | string | bool | choice | weighted_choice. More strategies (regex, custom, dependent) added in later slices.

values.description: Candidate set for choice / weighted_choice strategies.

weights.description: Per-value weights for weighted_choice. Length must match values.

distribution.description: Optional distribution hint for numeric strategies. Default: uniform.
